title: 2024 South Korean legislative election (English Wikipedia)
known_bias: English Wikipedia — editorial synthesis of multiple news and academic sources; internal citations not independently verified
notes: First-pass election-wide reference. Emphasizes green-onion incident and assassination attempt on Lee Jae-myung.

title: 대한민국 제22대 국회의원 선거 (Korean Wikipedia)
known_bias: Korean Wikipedia — editorial synthesis; not strongly partisan-aligned. Different emphasis than English coverage (names specific controversies; underweights single-incident narrative).
notes: Bias-pair companion to wiki-22-en. Names Chae Sang-byeol, Kim Keon-hee, Itaewon, 2023 Jamboree, Busan Expo controversies specifically. Flags record advance turnout (31.28%) and 2023 Gangseo-gu by-election as harbinger.

title: 대한민국 제21대 국회의원 선거 서울특별시 (Korean Wikipedia)
known_bias: Korean Wikipedia Seoul subpage — editorial synthesis; per-constituency table of winners, runners-up, vote shares.
notes: Source of truth for 2020 Seoul per-constituency winners + vote shares. Per-district turnout not included in source.

title: 종로구의 국회의원 (Korean Wikipedia)
known_bias: Korean Wikipedia constituency page — editorial consensus. '정치 1번지' label is widespread in Korean political discourse.
notes: Jongno identified as bellwether, '정치 1번지'. Notes Gwak Sang-eon 2024 as Roh Moo-hyun's son-in-law. No demographic analysis in source.

title: 강남구 갑 (Korean Wikipedia)
known_bias: Korean Wikipedia constituency page; focuses on winner succession and boundary changes, not voter analysis.
notes: Consistent PPP stronghold since 2000. 2024: 서명옥 64.18%. Geography: neighborhoods 신사, 논현, 압구정, 청담, 역삼.

title: 용산구 (1988년 선거구) (Korean Wikipedia)
known_bias: Korean Wikipedia constituency page; detailed margin history. Does not discuss 2022 presidential office relocation.
notes: Genuine swing district: 2000 margin 0.12pp, 2020 margin 0.66pp, historically flips between parties. 권영세 is recent PPP incumbent.

title: 마포구 갑 (Korean Wikipedia)
known_bias: Korean Wikipedia constituency page; no voter-analysis content.
notes: 2024 flipped PPP at 0.6pp margin after 20-year DPK streak. 조정훈 path: Transition Korea Party then PPP. Structural significance of flip unclear given tiny margin.

title: 동작구 을 (Korean Wikipedia)
known_bias: Korean Wikipedia constituency page; detailed Na Kyung-won record across 4 elections.
notes: 10+ point rightward swing 2020→2024 (45.04% → 54.01% for 나경원). Na Kyung-won is a top-tier national PPP figure. District 상도1, 흑석, 사당1-5동.

title: Opinion polling for the 2024 South Korean legislative election (English Wikipedia)
known_bias: English Wikipedia compilation of polling data from multiple Korean polling firms (Korea Research, Research View, MBC, Korea Gallup, Realmeter, etc.); reflects published numbers not editorial synthesis.
notes: Source of truth for vote-intention polling Jan-Apr 2024. DPK lead narrowed from 12pp to 6.58pp over the campaign. Does not include executive approval polling.

title: Yoon Suk Yeol (English Wikipedia)
known_bias: Biographical Wikipedia article; covers approval data unevenly with notable gaps for Itaewon, doctor strike, and 2024 campaign-period polling.
notes: Limited quantitative approval data: Aug 2022 (24%), Sept 2022 (27.7%). Confirms qualitative low-approval pattern but lacks comprehensive Korea Gallup / Realmeter timeline.

title: Seoul Halloween crowd crush (English Wikipedia)
known_bias: Detailed factual reconstruction with multiple Korean and international source citations; relatively neutral tone.
notes: 159 deaths; Nov 2022 poll showed 7 of 10 Koreans held government responsible. 23 officers charged. Used for the scandal-chain article.

title: Death of Chae Su-geun (English Wikipedia)
known_bias: English Wikipedia summary of the 채상병 (Corporal Chae) investigation-pressure controversy; some details thin.
notes: 2023-07-19 Marine death during flood rescue; Defense Minister halted handover to police. Lee Jong-sup resigned ambassadorship within weeks of March 2024 appointment.

title: Rebuilding Korea Party (English Wikipedia)
known_bias: Standard party-page coverage; founder Cho Kuk's legal context covered factually.
notes: Founded 2024-03-03; 24.25% PR vote (6.87M votes), 12 PR seats, 0 constituency candidates by design. Used for opposition_unity and party_fragmentation articles.

title: Lee Jun-seok (English Wikipedia)
known_bias: Biographical page; covers PPP suspension and Reform Party founding factually.
notes: Founded Reform Party 2024-01-20 after Dec 2023 PPP departure. Won Hwaseong B with 42.41% (51,856 votes). *Idaenam* young-male voter base. Used for party_fragmentation.
